OBJECTIONS TO DECLARATION OF JON-MARC DOBRIN (TRANSACTION ID # 100011983) FILED BY DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY

LAW AND MOTION 302, REPLY EVIDENCE WAS NOT CONSIDERED. DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE BASED ON IMPROPER VENUE. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATIVE REQUEST TO CHANGE VENUE BASED ON INCONVENIENCE TO WITNESSES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO BEING REFILED. CRUSADER FAILS TO SHOW THAT ITS LIABILITY DID NOT ARISE IN SAN FRANCISCO, WHICH IS A PERMISSIBLE BASIS FOR VENUE PER CCP 395.5. "THE LIABILITY ARISES WHERE THE INJURY OCCURS'THE WORD INJURY 'MEANS A WRONGFUL INVASION OF LEGAL RIGHTS.'" (TOKUZO SHIDA V. JAPAN FOOD CORP. (1960) 185 CAL.APP.2D 443, 448.) IN THIS BAD FAITH ACTION, PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE THAT CRUSADER ENGAGED IN BAD FAITH BY SETTLING IN FAVOR OF EXTREME TOWING, INC. TO THE DETRIMENT OF MR. MARENTES. THE DECLARATION OF JON-MARC DOBRIN SHOWS THAT THE BAD FAITH CONDUCT OCCURRED IN SAN FRANCISCO AND CRUSADER DOES NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE TO THE CONTRARY. CRUSADER'S REQUEST TO CHANGE VENUE BASED ON THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS NOT AMENABLE TO BEING GRANTED. FIRST, THE NOTICE OF MOTION DOES NOT CITE CCP 397 OR THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES AS A BASIS FOR THE MOTION. SECOND, SUCH A MOTION CAN ONLY BE FILED AFTER THE CASE IS AT ISSUE. (BURAN EQUIPMENT CO., INC. V. SUP. CRT. (1987) 190 CAL.APP.3D 1662, 1665). THIRD, CRUSADER MAKES AN INSUFFICIENT SUBSTANTIVE SHOWING REGARDING WHICH WITNESSES WILL BE INCONVENIENCED. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS OFF CALENDAR. THE COURT ENTERED JUDGMENT IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY ON AUGUST 7, 2019. THE COURT THEREFORE NO LONGER HAS THE POWER TO RULE ON THE RECONSIDERATION MOTION. (SEE RAMON V. AEROSPACE CORP. (1996) 50 CAL.APP.4TH 1233, 1236 ["[A]FTER ENTRY OF JUDGMENT, A TRIAL COURT HAS NO FURTHER POWER TO RULE ON A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION."].) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For New Trial & Payment Of Court Reporter Fee
Off Calendar Oct-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff Liudmila Bichegkeuva's motion for new trial is denied.

On July 10, 2019, the Court granted defendant Crusader Insurance Company's motion for summary judgment and denied plaintiffs' motion for summary adjudication. On August 7, 2019, judgment was entered in favor of defendant. Defendant served plaintiff by mail with notice of entry of that judgment on August 8, 2019. On August 28, 2019, the Court took off calendar plaintiff's motion for reconsideration. (See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236 ["after entry of judgment, a trial court has no further power to rule on a motion for reconsideration"].)

Plaintiff did not file the statutorily-mandated notice of intention to move for a new trial within 15 days of the date of mailing notice of entry of judgment, or by August 23, 2019. (Code Civ. Proc. 659(a).) Nor did plaintiff, within 10 days of filing such a notice, serve and file the supporting brief and accompanying documents. (Id. 659a.) Instead, on September 9, 2019, plaintiff filed the instant motion for a new trial. The Court lacks jurisdiction to rule on plaintiff's belated motion. (Tri-County Elevator Co. v. Superior Court (1982) 135 Cal.App.3d 271, 277 ["Because compliance with the 15-day requirement of section 659 is jurisdictional [citations], respondent court was without power to entertain the motion [for new trial] and properly denied it."]; Douglas v. Janis (1974) 43 Cal.App.3d 931, 936 [because compliance with the 15-day requirement of section 659 is "jurisdictional," a late notice is "totally ineffectual" and a trial court has "no power to entertain or act on the motion"].) In any event, plaintiffs fail to show that a new trial is warranted, either on the grounds of newly discovered evidence or of an error in law. (Code Civ. Proc. 657(4),(7).)

Counsel for the defendant is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs motion for reconsideration is off calendar. The court entered judgment in favor of defendant Crusader Insurance Company on August 7, 2019. The court therefore no longer has the power to rule on the reconsideration motion. (See Ramon v. Aerospace Corp. (1996) 50 Cal.App.4th 1233, 1236 ["[A]fter entry of judgment, a trial court has no further power to rule on a motion for reconsideration."].)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Set For Trial On May-29-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar Mar-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jon-Marc Dobrin, of Law Offices of Jon-Marc Dobrin, Tel. 415.459.0307, for plaintiffs Liudmila Bichegkueva;
Joseph E. Foss, of Sheppard Mullin Richter & Hampton, LLP, Tel. 619.338.6647, for defendants Crusader Insurance Company, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Reply evidence was not considered. DEFENDANT CRUSADER INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Transfer Venue based on improper venue is denied and its alternative request to change venue based on inconvenience to witnesses is denied without prejudice to being refiled. Crusader fails to show that its liability did not arise in San Francisco, which is a permissible basis for venue per CCP 395.5. "The liability arises where the injury occurs'The word injury 'means a wrongful invasion of legal rights.'" (Tokuzo Shida v. Japan Food Corp. (1960) 185 Cal.App.2d 443, 448.) In this bad faith action, plaintiffs allege that Crusader engaged in bad faith by settling in favor of Extreme Towing, Inc. to the detriment of Mr. Marentes. The declaration of Jon-Marc Dobrin shows that the bad faith conduct occurred in San Francisco and Crusader does not present any evidence to the contrary. Crusader's request to change venue based on the convenience of witnesses is not amenable to being granted. First, the notice of motion does not cite CCP 397 or the convenience of witnesses as a basis for the motion. Second, such a motion can only be filed after the case is at issue. (Buran Equipment Co., Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1987) 190 Cal.App.3d 1662, 1665). Third, Crusader makes an insufficient substantive showing regarding which witnesses will be inconvenienced. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
